description: This unbaked wine in a can tastes a bit tired, but it has an earthy appeal. The color is a brownish red and the aromas seem dusty and mature. The flavors are beefy and peppery while the texture is nicely tannic.
title: Sans Wine Co 2016 Poor Ranch Vineyards Zinfandel (Mendocino County)

description: Made from Loire Valley fruit, this has the tangy, herbal character typical of cool-climate wines. Drink now.
title: Villebois 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Vin de France)

description: Aromas of honeysuckle and exotic fruit lead the way. The aromas flow over to the rather sweet supple palate along with mature white peach, candied lemon peel and a confectionery note. A soft mousse backs up the rich flavors.
title: Col Vetoraz 2016 Dry  (Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze)

description: Hints of smoke and earth on the nose lead to a riper and sweeter, approachably fruity palate. It's a light bodied spätlese, easy drinking, with loads of sunny, sweet white grapefruit and tangerine flavors. Drink now.
title: Heinz Eifel 2016 Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: This refreshing, well-made wine combines vibrant citrus flavors with richer ripe melon and apple notes for an interesting and enjoyable personality.
title: Husch 2016 La Ribera Vineyards Sauvignon Blanc (Mendocino)

description: This bottling shows off both sides of Sauvignon Blanc—at once lush and tropical and racy and crisp. Ripe Gala apple and Bosc pear aromas meet tart orange peel and rubber on the nose. The palate is bolstered by energetic acidity, with a stony texture and flavors of sour Granny Smith apple and yellow grapefruit.
title: Ancient Peaks 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Margarita Ranch)

description: The merest touch of rhubarb shows on the nose. The palate is similarly restrained in flavor, offering some tart red-apple notes and lively, frothy foam. The body is light and just about dry. Drink this easy, frothy sparkler soon.
title: Markus Huber 2016 Hugo Rosé Sparkling Zweigelt (Weinland Österreich)

description: Luscious grapefruit and lychee juxtapose earthier notes of saffron and fennel that extend from nose to finish. It's a dry, full-bodied Gewürztraminer with sunny acidity lending freshness to the midpalate. Drink now through 2019.
title: Three Brothers 2016 Lonesome Stony Estate Reserve Gewürztraminer (Finger Lakes)

description: Lean and graceful, this appealing wine from a Rhône grape variety blends fresh, appley aromas and crisp, dry jicama and pippin apple flavors with a firm and refreshing mouthfeel. It's light-bodied and well-balanced.
title: Acquiesce 2016 Picpoul (Lodi)

description: Hints of ripe Mirabelle and yellow apple show on the rather restrained nose. The palate adds bright, lemony freshness and zesty, pleasantly bitter citrus pith to the fruit, creating a texture alive with juiciness and shot throught with freshness. It leaves a lasting, lemony aftertaste.
title: Kracher 2016 Pinot Gris (Burgenland)

description: This late harvest wine from Hungary is comprised of Furmint, Hárslevelu and Muscat. It has aromas of acacia flower and pear, with flavors of apricot, pear juice and dried rosemary. The finish is pleasantly sweet without being too cloying.
title: Royal Tokaji 2016 Late Harvest  (Tokaji)

description: Aromas of charcuterie, smoke and grilled herb lead the nose. On the simple palate, a note of white pepper accents a red-berry core. It's easy drinking, with fresh acidity and soft tannins.
title: Aldegheri 2016 Tenuta Villa Cariola  (Bardolino)

description: This is an over-the-top vintage for this wine; it's simply crammed full with floral and fresh fruit flavors. Grapefruit, peach and melon notes are in abundance, all juicy, ripe and resonant. It would be hard to imagine a better or more versatile non-Chardonnay white at this price.
title: Abacela 2016 Albariño (Umpqua Valley)

description: Kevin Riley packs eight varieties into this pink wine, but the major proportion of Syrah and Grenache lend it its deep hue and rose petal, red cherry and vanilla notes on the nose. Grippy red plum skin flavors dominate the compellingly tense palate, followed by Rainier cherry and orange rind touches.
title: Proulx 2016 Resistance Willow Creek Farm Rosé (Paso Robles)

description: The ripeness of Mercurey in this vintage is very apparent in this rich, smooth and soft wine. Juicy red fruits dominate, backed by a core of tannins and the spice from wood aging. It is a wine with a good medium-term future. Drink from 2019.
title: Vignerons de Buxy 2016 Buissonnier  (Mercurey)

description: This wine comes from a selection of the best concrete egg, oak and stainless-steel-fermented selections of this vineyard-designate, combined with 3% Sémillon. It shows a golden honeyed touch of oak and a rich nose, notions that follow on the palate. Full bodied and lengthy, it holds highlights of lemon, quince and ginger.
title: V. Sattui 2016 Carsi Vineyard Reserve Stock Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: A very pale peach color, this wine brings bright aromas of strawberry Jolly Rancher, cherry and kiwi. It drinks just a hair off dry, with tart fruit flavors, providing plenty of easy-drinking pleasure.
title: Airfield Estates 2016 Sangiovese Rosé (Yakima Valley)

description: An unusual but alluring perfume of boxwood and spicy grapefruit zest rises from the glass. The palate follows on light feet, with a delicate, translucent body that shimmers in green citrus and spice tones. This wine is dry, light but totally intriguing, with a final note of pure lemon.
title: Wohlmuth 2016 Kitzecker Riesling (Südsteiermark)

description: Strawberry and watermelon highlight a light, creamy palate of vanilla and lemon peel, the freshness maintained by moderate acidity.
title: Kokomo 2016 Pauline's Vineyard Grenache Rosé (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Green apple appears amidst the more usual yellow and green-pear fruit and sets a fresh accent. The palate carries through on these vivid fruit notes and adds yeasty, savory spice: white pepper and arugula leaf create real interest. it's very refreshing and totally moreish.
title: Hiedler 2016 Langenlois Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: A tinge of copper to this white's hue suggests a bit of skin contact, and there's a decided textural element and tannic grip on the palate as well. Pear and melon flavors finish refreshing and mouthwatering in spite of the wine's richness. Drink now.
title: Brotte 2016 Les Hauts de Barville White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: The wine comes from a new appellation at the southern end of the Côte de Beaune. It is a blend of different premier cru vineyards to give a juicy raspberry flavored. Tannins show a solid dry background to the attractive perfumed fruitiness. Drink from 2019.
title: Jean-Luc and Paul Aegerter 2016 Premier Cru  (Maranges)

description: This is a stalky light wine. With black-currant fruit and layers of intense acidity, it is fruity with a cool crisp aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Domaine de Pellehaut 2016 Harmonie de Gascogne Red (Côtes de Gascogne)

description: Although it is not indicated on the label, the fruit for this Jean-Pierre Fayard family wine comes from the La Londe cru region. The wine with its organically grown grapes is complex, ripe and fruity. It has great elegance and a stylish character that comes from the cool collected acidity and red-fruit flavors. Drink this wine now.
title: Château Sainte Marguerite 2016 Cuvée Symphonie Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: The aromas are bright and fragrant, with notes of pear, flowers, star fruit and mandarin-orange rind. The palate is full of tropical fruit and orange flavors that provide appeal.
title: 3 Horse Ranch Vineyards 2016 Single Vineyard Pinot Gris

description: Dark-skinned berry and culinary spice aromas float from the glass. The straightforward savory palate evokes black cherry, cassis and ground clove accompanied by polished tannins.
title: Acinum 2016  Valpolicella

description: This is almost red in color, with spicy bell pepper aromas resulting from early harvested Cabernet Sauvignon. Its full, chunky palate shows yeasty, lightly green and peppery flavors that linger on the finish.
title: Príncipe de Viana 2016 Cabernet Sauvignon Rosado (Navarra)

description: The wine is crisp and as light as a feather. The fruitiness brings with it apple and pink grapefruit as well as warmer golden plums and a perfumed aftertaste. Drink the wine now.
title: DFJ Vinhos 2016 Coreto Branco White (Lisboa)

description: This broad, soft wine has hints of spice as well as ripe apricot flavors. The texture is easy and attractive. Drink now.
title: Encosta do Sobral 2016 Reserva Branco White (Tejo)

description: Intensely crisp and perfumed, this is a green-fruit flavored wine. Its tangy character is reinforced by a mineral texture. The wine needs another six months, so drink from 2018.
title: Casal da Coelheira 2016 Private Collection Verdelho (Tejo)

description: Bursting with ripe apple scents and flavors, this is dry and ready to enjoy. Fermented in a mix of neutral barrel and stainless steel, it starts off sharp and assertive but mellows into pleasing roundness on the midpalate. Drink now and over the next year or two while it's at its freshest.
title: Chehalem 2016 Ridgecrest Vineyards Grüner Veltliner (Ribbon Ridge)

description: This wine is rich and packed with black fruits. The soft texture and ripe fruitiness mean the tannins will soon fully integrate. Drink from 2018.
title: Herdade do Rocim 2016 Mariana Tinto Red (Alentejano)

description: This light-colored, alert wine with melon and nectarine aromas is confident up front. Cleansing acidity is the driver of a juicy, tangy and lean palate. Crisp flavors of pie cherry, currant and rose water finish fresh.
title: Volver 2016 Tarima Rosado (Alicante)

description: This is a structured and balanced wine. It has spice along with tannins that are built on ripe red-fruits. This is a full-bodied wine that is made for food. Drink now.
title: Château Turcaud 2016  Bordeaux Clairet

description: Brightly fruity, this blend with 85% Sauvignon Blanc goes for the tropical-fruit character of the grape. Attractive acidity and a zingy edge are balanced by ripe apricot and pineapple flavors. Drink from late 2017.
title: Château Roquefort 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Vegetal-leaning aromas of beans, peas and squash lead to a round somewhat flabby palate. Green, herbal flavors crowd out mild citrus and melon notes in front of a low-acid soft finish.
title: Santa Ema 2016 Amplus Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

description: Tropical fruit aromas meld with a whiff of Mediterranean herb. The bright, simple palate shows hints of banana and citrus, alongside zesty acidity.
title: Tenute Orestiadi 2016 Molino a Vento Grillo (Terre Siciliane)

description: This is a really light wine with bright fruit and a crisp texture. It has a breezy feel—all crisp fruit and lively acidity. Drink now.
title: Thomas Carteron 2016 Le Bonheur de Provence Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Slightly gritty dusty aromas are low on fruit. This feels plump and round, with modest acidity. Simple melon and mild citrus flavors finish with pyrazine-driven notes of bell pepper and jalapeño.
title: Echeverria 2016 Reserva Unwooded Chardonnay (Curicó Valley)

description: Somewhat flat and semi-sweet aromas of uncarbonated cider, yellow pear and white peach show on the nose. Similar sour apple and cooked stone-fruit flavors show on the sip.
title: Minassian-Young 2016 Alvarinho (Paso Robles)

description: This is a light- to medium-bodied rosé made exclusively from Syrah grown in the northern reaches of the Rhône Valley. The cooler climate gives it a lighter bearing and redder fruit flavors than many other Rhône rosés: this one resembles red plum and pineapple. Drink now.
title: Cave de Tain 2016 Première Note Rosé de Syrah (Collines Rhôdaniennes)

description: Prickly, sharp aromas of citrus, matchstick and haystack are low on fruit. This Verdejo has a sharp acidic feel and pyrazinic flavors of cactus and bell pepper along with standard citrus.
title: Viña Mayor 2016 Verdejo (Rueda)

description: With plenty of acidity and tannins, this wine is almost trying to be a young red. Drink it like that with food and it will be worth it. The aftertaste has pepper and attractive spice.
title: Château de Marsan 2016  Bordeaux Clairet

description: Sometimes less is more, and sometimes more is more, as in this 68,000-case offering from A to Z. It's a leesy, pretty, highly gulpable wine, loaded with apple, pear and melon fruit. Hints of cucumber and stone add freshness to the lingering aftertaste.
title: A to Z 2016 Pinot Gris (Oregon)
